category_title: Students with EBD experiencing behavioral outbursts and reading difficulties in science
minimal_context: Sam is asked to read aloud from the science textbook in front of the other students in his science class.
dialogue:
TUTOR: Sam, it is your turn to read the next paragraph from the science textbook aloud to the class.

STUDENT: I don't want to. I have trouble reading this textbook.

TUTOR: Just give it a try and read the first sentence.

STUDENT: No, when I read aloud, the other students make fun of me.

TUTOR: Nobody is making fun of you. Please just read the text.

STUDENT: I said no! You are all making fun of me! Shut up!

options.A: Implement a progressive disciplinary sequence: first a warning, then a 10-minute timeout, followed by detention or suspension.
options.B: Highlight crucial points in the science textbook that will later appear in the student's examinations.
options.C: Introduce the student to alternative science books that are of an appropriate reading and engagement level.
options.D: Adopt inquiry methods to teach science instead of using traditional textbook and lecture approaches.
